gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Map a 1-based `Pos` (line/col) to a byte offset in `src`, using a precomputed `line_starts`."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " The lexer counts `col` in characters (1-based), so we walk `col - 1` chars from the line start to"] # [doc = " land on the correct byte offset (handles multi-byte UTF-8). A position past end-of-line clamps to"] # [doc = " the source length — never panics (G2)."] fn pos_to_byte (line_starts : & [usize] , src : & str , pos : Pos) -> usize { let line_idx = (pos . line as usize) . saturating_sub (1) ; let Some (& line_byte) = line_starts . get (line_idx) else { return src . len () ; } ; let col_offset = (pos . col as usize) . saturating_sub (1) ; let rest = & src [line_byte ..] ; match rest . char_indices () . nth (col_offset) { Some ((byte_in_line , _)) => line_byte + byte_in_line , None => { line_starts . get (line_idx + 1) . map_or (src . len () , | & next | next . saturating_sub (1)) } } }
gaps.reason: Rust `.saturating_sub` is saturating (silent clamp) — Mycelium has no saturating prim surface; desugaring to `saturating_sub(…)` would fabricate an unknown prim or lie about overflow (G2/VR-5). Gap never-silently (express gap-close 2026-07-16).
